content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE MAYOR
SAN FRANCISCO

GEORGE R. MOSCONE

[handwritten stamp: MAR 1 11 16 AM 77]

[handwritten: A-77-405]

[handwritten: Garber, Edward R.]

Sacramento, California
February 28, 1977

Speaker Leo McCarthy
Assemblyman Willie Brown
Assemblyman Arthur Agnos
Senator Milton Marks
Senator John Foran

Re: Legislation protecting disclosure of Attorneys
Clients and Fees paid

Gentlemen:

The City and County of San Francisco has determined
that there is a conflict between the disclosure
requirement imposed upon attorneys under the Political
Reform Act and the statutory obligation of an attorney
to his or her client. Basically this is due to the
following:

1. Government Code 87207 (b) (2) requires public
officials or designated employees, including
attorneys to disclose the names of clients
who paid fees over $1,000.

2. However, Business and Professional Code 6068 (c)
requires attorneys to "maintain, inviolate the
confidence, and at every peril to himself to
preserve the secrets, of his clients".

By the attached resolution San Francisco is urging
the legislature to correct this infringement upon the
attorney-client relationship by deleting Government
Code 87207 (b) (2) and limiting the applicability

Page 2                                February 28, 1977


of 87207 (b) (3) to those business entities which
are not legally bound to protect confidential rela-
tionships.

Any assistance you can provide will be greatly
appreciated.  If I can provide you with more infor-
mation, please let me know.

                                        Sincerely yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Edward R. Gerber
                                        State Representative

ERG:dcj

cc:  Richard E. Watson
     Donald C. Benninghoven
     Daniel Lowenstein

RESOLUTION NO. 23-77

URGING THE STATE LEGISLATURE TO ENACT LEGISLATION PROTECTING FROM
DISCLOSURE REQUIRED BY THE POLITICAL REFORM ACT THE IDENTITY OF
CLIENTS OF ATTORNEYS AND FEES PAID.

WHEREAS, Government Code Section 87207 (b)(2), commonly
referred to as the Political Reform Act, requires attorneys, who,
because they are public officials or designated employees of a
public entity, to disclose, if said person must disclose sources
of income, the name of every person who paid fees to the attorney
if the attorney's pro rata share of the fee was at least $1,000.00;
and,

WHEREAS, Business and Professions Code Section 6068(e) im-
poses upon attorneys the duty to "maintain inviolate the confidence
and at every peril to himself to preserve the secrets, of his
clients;" and,

WHEREAS, The mandatory disclosure of an attorney's clients
may conflict with his duty to maintain confidential relationship;
and,

WHEREAS, The Political Reform Act unfairly discriminates
against attorneys and brokers by singling those professions out
for client disclosure not required of other individual service
providers whose sources of income are to be disclosed; and,

WHEREAS, The requirement that attorneys must disclose clients
if they are required to disclose sources of income violates the
Fourteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution by depriving,
without due process of law, an attorney's right and duty to protect
confidential legal relationships, by placing unreasonable limita-
tions on a public official's or employee's ability to enter into
confidential legal relationships, and by applying the obligations
of disclosure so unequally as to infringe, unjustifiably, upon

the rights of attorneys to pursue their profession; now, therefore,
be it

RESOLVED, That this Board of Supervisors hereby urge the
Legislature of the State of California to amend Government Code
Section 87207 by deleting subsection (b)(2) and by limiting the
application of subsection (b)(3) to those business entities which
are not legally bound to protect confidential relationships.

APPROVED AS TO FORM AND
RECOMMENDED:

THOMAS M. O'CONNOR, City Attorney

By_______________________
    Deputy City Attorney

Adopted on date of Introduction - Board of Supervisors, San Francisco.    JAN 31 1977

Ayes: Supervisors Bataglida, Feinstein, [illegible], Gonzalez, Kopp, Molinari, Nelder & [illegible].

Absent: Supervisors [illegible], [illegible], [illegible], [illegible], [illegible].

I hereby certify that the foregoing resolution was adopted by the
Board of Supervisors of the City and County of San Francisco.

_________________________
Clerk

12-277-6
File No.                    Approved

_________________________
Mayor
